nativeRange.habitat: Shallow tropical reef slopes, lagoons, bays, and boat channels; typically found on rock and rubble in lower to middle reef zones with reduced light and moderate nutrient levels

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae. Absorbs dissolved organic nutrients from the water column. Benefits from supplemental spot feeding of small meaty foods — brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, or micro-plankton — approximately once or twice per week. Feeding is optional but tends to improve growth and coloration, particularly in low-nutrient systems.

goodWith: clownfish (may use as host anemone substitute)
other Rhodactis mushrooms with adequate spacing
avoidWith: aggressive LPS corals in direct contact (torch, hammer, frogspawn, chalice)
large sweeper-tentacle SPS corals without spacing
large urchins that may dislodge frags
digging or sand-sifting fish near placement

summary: Rhodactis mushroom corals are a genus of beginner-friendly corallimorpharians prized for vivid colorations. They generally thrive under low to moderate light and low flow on lower rockwork; suitable for most established reef systems.
careNotes:
Rhodactis is a genus of corallimorph corals in Order Corallimorpharia, Family Discosomatidae. Unlike true stony corals, they lack a calcium carbonate skeleton. The genus is commonly sold in the trade under names such as 'Bullseye Mushroom,' 'Superman Mushroom,' and various color-trade names. This entry covers the non-hairy Rhodactis trade (principally R. inchoata and R. mussoides); the sibling entry 'Hairy Mushroom Coral' covers R. indosinensis.

Species notes:
- R. inchoata (Carlgren, 1943): The most commonly sold 'Bullseye Mushroom' or 'Blue Mushroom.' Smaller polyps, typically 5–15 cm diameter. Known for bold circular patterning.
- R. mussoides (Duchassaing & Michelotti, 1864): Larger, somewhat folded polyps reaching 20–25 cm diameter. Olive green with white/pink oral area; sometimes has a hairy texture distinct from R. indosinensis.
- R. musciformis (syn. R. osculifera in some literature): Caribbean-range species, less common in trade.
- Many specimens sold simply as 'Rhodactis sp.' cannot be reliably identified to species level from photos alone; genus-level treatment is appropriate for general husbandry.

Lighting: Low to moderate PAR is generally advised (approximately 50–150 PAR). Most sources place the sweet spot at 75–100 PAR. New specimens should be acclimated gradually — starting lower and moving up if coloration or disc expansion is poor.

Flow: Low flow is preferred. Direct powerhead current has been documented causing tissue stress, shrinking, and detachment. Indirect or gentle alternating flow is recommended.

Placement: Lower to middle tank zones are generally advised, where light and flow are gentler.

Aggression: Semi-aggressive — has been known to sting and overgrow neighboring corals given direct contact. Maintain clear spacing from LPS and other corals. In high-density mushroom tanks, allelopathic chemical release may affect neighbors; running activated carbon is sometimes recommended.

Predation: Rhodactis species can engulf small invertebrates and food items by expanding and closing their disc. Generally does not pose a threat to passing fish under normal conditions.

Calcium and alkalinity requirements are lower than stony corals since no skeletal deposition occurs; stable parameters matter more than precision highs.

Handling: Standard reef precautions apply (gloves recommended, wash hands after contact). Rhodactis possess nematocysts that can cause skin irritation.

Reproduction: Reproduces asexually via longitudinal fission and pedal laceration. Fragging by cutting is practiced in the hobby; cut pieces can be placed on rubble or frag plugs and will typically attach within days.

Host behavior: Clownfish have been documented using large Rhodactis colonies as anemone substitutes, though this is less common than with R. indosinensis.

Temperature source spread: TFH Magazine (R. mussoides) gives 72–83°F (22–28°C); LiveAquaria gives 72–78°F (22–26°C); Reeflings gives 24–28°C; Extreme Corals and Sunnyside Corals give 75–80°F (24–27°C). Averaged range 23–27°C is used; the higher end (28°C) may be tolerated for the R. mussoides species specifically.

Adult size spread: R. inchoata typically 4–8 inches (10–20 cm); R. mussoides can reach 8–10 inches (20–25 cm) or slightly larger. Genus entry uses 5–25 cm to span the trade range.
breedingNotes: Reproduces asexually in captivity via longitudinal fission (developing two mouths, then dividing) and pedal laceration (fragments of the basal disc developing into new polyps). Fragging by cutting mature specimens is a commonly practiced propagation method. Cut pieces are typically placed on rubble or frag plugs in a low-flow area with indirect light to recover. Sexual reproduction occurs on natural reefs but is not practiced in the hobby.

primary.name: LiveAquaria – Purple Bullseye Rhodactis Mushroom Coral
primary.notes: Primary species page for Rhodactis inchoata (Purple Bullseye). Provided temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), SG (1.023–1.025), dKH (8–12), care level (Easy), placement (Bottom), flow (Low), temperament (Semi-Aggressive), diet (zooxanthellae + micro-plankton/brine shrimp), adult size (4 inches diameter).

additional.name: TFH Magazine – Rhodactis mussoides
additional.notes: Cross-checked R. mussoides specifically. Temperature 72–83°F (22–28°C), pH 8.1–8.2, salinity 1.025, calcium 380–430 ppm, alkalinity 2.5 meq/L (~7 dKH), low-moderate light with some current required. Adult size 8–10 inches diameter. Native range: tropical Indo-Pacific reef slopes, bays, lagoons. Confirmed Family Discosomatidae, Order Corallimorpharia.

additional.name: Top Shelf Aquatics – Mushroom Coral Care Guide
additional.notes: Cross-checked genus-level Rhodactis parameters. Temperature 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, alkalinity 8–12 dKH, calcium 400–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1350 ppm. Flow low to moderate; feeding once or twice per week. Family Discosomatidae confirmed.

additional.name: Extreme Corals – Rhodactis Mushroom Care Guide
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters and care: temperature 76–80°F (24–27°C), SG 1.024–1.026, pH 8.0–8.4, alkalinity 7–10 dKH, PAR 75–150. Beginner-to-intermediate care level. Low to moderate indirect flow preferred. Supplemental feeding 1–2x/week noted.

additional.name: Tidal Gardens – Rhodactis Mushroom Coral Care
additional.notes: Confirmed PAR range 75–150, native range (Indo-Pacific: Fiji, Tonga, Solomon Islands, Great Barrier Reef), size range 1–18 inches across varieties, asexual reproduction via longitudinal fission. Taxonomy: Order Corallimorpharia.
